flag: "tai maanantaisin tammikuussa" after a specific date feels abrupt; a Finn would likely say "tai joka maanantai tammikuussa". Also, "keskiyöllä" placement at the very end is fine but the whole sentence is long and slightly confusing.

"5 minuutin kohdalla klo 9.05" is redundant and contradictory — 5 min past the hour at 9:05 makes sense only on the :05 mark, but saying "5 minuutin kohdalla" and then listing "klo 9.05–17.05" is oddly phrased and hard to parse. The sentence is very long and confusing.
"tammikuun ja heinäkuun 1. päivänä" is grammatically awkward — a Finn would say "tammikuun 1. ja heinäkuun 1. päivänä" or rephrase. The sentence is also very long with multiple clauses.
"klo 9.00–9.59" is unusual; a Finn would more naturally say "klo 9" or "klo 9–10". The precise minute range feels technical/machine-generated.

"klo 9.00–9.59 ja 17.00–17.59" — same issue as id 5, very technical hour ranges. Also "sunnuntaisin ja lauantaisin" would more naturally be "lauantaisin ja sunnuntaisin" (Sat before Sun is more common in Finnish).

Extremely long and hard to parse. "klo 0.00–0.59, 5.00–5.59, 10.00–10.59, 15.00–15.59 ja 20.00–20.59" is a very unnatural way to express hour windows in everyday Finnish. The dual condition (date range OR weekday) tacked on at the end adds further confusion.
"tasatunnista alkaen" is slightly formal/technical but understandable. "kuukauden 1. päivänä tai perjantaisin" is a clear enough structure.
Same issue as id 7 — listing five separate hour windows as "klo 0.00–0.59, 5.00–5.59..." is not how a Finn would naturally express this. Very hard to read.
"klo 0.00–0.30" would more naturally be "klo 0.00–0.30" — acceptable, but a Finn might just say "puoleen yöhön asti". Otherwise fine.
"0–30 minuutin kohdalla" is awkward — in natural Finnish you'd say "0–30. minuutilla" or more likely just describe it differently. The comma before "klo" is also slightly off in rhythm.
"joka kolmannen kuukauden" is understandable but slightly stiff. Listing four specific months after explaining the pattern is redundant and confusing. Very long sentence.
Listing specific times "klo 9.05, 9.10, 9.30, 17.05, 17.10 ja 17.30" is clear and readable. The "tai perjantaisin" condition is natural.
"klo 9–20 ja 22" is an odd range — listing hour 22 separately after a range feels unnatural. The rest is okay but the sentence is long.

"5 ja 30 sekunnin kohdalla" combined with "15 minuutin välein" is very hard to parse — seconds and minutes mixed together in one sentence is confusing. The structure does not read naturally in Finnish.
